text: Pascal's law states that pressure applied to an enclosed fluid:
options: Acts only at the point of application
Is transmitted equally in all directions throughout the fluid
explanation: Pascal's Law: pressure applied to a confined fluid is transmitted undiminished in all directions (hydraulic press principle).
name: Pascal's Law

text: The pressure at depth \(h\) in a liquid of density \(\rho\) is:
explanation: Hydrostatic pressure: \( P = P_0 + \rho g h \), where \(P_0\) = atmospheric pressure at surface.
name: Pressure at Depth

text: Archimedes' principle states that the buoyant force equals:
options: Weight of the fluid displaced by the object
Pressure at the bottom of object
explanation: Archimedes' Principle: \( F_b = \rho_{fluid} V_{submerged} g \) = weight of displaced fluid. Object floats if \(F_b \geq W\).
name: Archimedes' Principle

text: Bernoulli's equation for steady flow is:
explanation: Bernoulli's equation: \( P + \frac{1}{2}\rho v^2 + \rho g h = \text{const} \). Energy conservation for ideal fluid flow.
name: Bernoulli's Equation

text: The equation of continuity for incompressible fluid flow is:
explanation: Continuity equation: \( A_1v_1 = A_2v_2 \) (volume flow rate = constant). Narrower tube → higher velocity.
name: Equation of Continuity

options: Cohesive forces between surface molecules being unbalanced
explanation: Surface tension: molecules on surface have unbalanced (net inward) cohesive forces → surface acts like a stretched membrane.
name: Origin of Surface Tension

text: The excess pressure inside a soap bubble of radius \(R\) is:
explanation: Soap bubble has 2 surfaces: \( \Delta P = \frac{4T}{R} \). Single liquid drop: \( \Delta P = \frac{2T}{R} \).
name: Excess Pressure in Soap Bubble

text: The height \(h\) of liquid rise in a capillary tube of radius \(r\) is:
explanation: Capillary rise: \( h = \frac{2T\cos\theta}{\rho g r} \). Water (\(\theta<90°\)) rises; mercury (\(\theta>90°\)) depresses.
name: Capillary Rise Formula

text: Stokes' law gives the viscous drag force on a sphere of radius \(r\) moving at speed \(v\) as:
explanation: Stokes' law: \( F = 6\pi\eta r v \). At terminal velocity: \( 6\pi\eta r v_t = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3(\rho - \sigma)g \).
name: Stokes' Law

text: Reynolds number (Re) determines the nature of fluid flow. Turbulent flow occurs when:
explanation: Re = \(\rho vD/\eta\). Re < 2000: laminar. 2000–3000: transition. Re > 3000: turbulent flow.
name: Reynolds Number

text: The terminal velocity of a sphere falling through a viscous fluid is proportional to:
explanation: Terminal velocity: \( v_t = \frac{2r^2(\rho-\sigma)g}{9\eta} \propto r^2 \). Larger sphere falls faster.
name: Terminal Velocity

text: The lift on an aircraft wing is explained by:
options: Bernoulli's principle: faster flow on curved upper surface creates lower pressure → net upward force
explanation: Aerofoil lift: Bernoulli's principle. Air flows faster over curved top → lower pressure above wing → pressure difference creates lift.
name: Aerofoil Lift

text: Viscosity of a liquid can be determined by:
options: Poiseuille's formula: \( Q = \frac{\pi r^4 \Delta P}{8\eta L} \)
explanation: Poiseuille's formula: volume flow rate \( Q = \frac{\pi r^4 \Delta P}{8\eta L} \). Used to measure viscosity of liquids.
name: Poiseuille's Formula

text: A body will float in a liquid if:
options: Its density is greater than liquid density
Its weight equals or is less than buoyant force (density ≤ liquid density)
explanation: Floating condition: \( W \leq F_b \), i.e., \( \rho_{body} \leq \rho_{liquid} \). If \(\rho_{body} = \rho_{liquid}\): body submerged but in equilibrium.
name: Floating Condition

text: The work done in blowing a soap bubble of radius \(R\) is:
explanation: Soap bubble has 2 surfaces, each of area \(4\pi R^2\). Total area \( = 8\pi R^2 \). Work \( W = T \times \Delta A = 8\pi R^2 T \).
name: Work in Blowing Soap Bubble
